title: Isolation of nuclei from fresh frozen brain sections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"506if","text":"This protocol is for the isolation of nuclei from fresh frozen brain sections that have been placed on slides. It's main steps consist of homogenization, washing, and isolation with an iodixanol. Once nuclei have been isolated, they should be processed according to the manufacturer's protocol for sequencing. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start:
{"blocks":[{"key":"c1p4b","text":"IMPORTANT: primate and human brain tissue are potentially infectious. Collect all contaminated
instruments, discarded tissue, and nuclei in a beaker to be bleached at the end
of the experiment. Decontaminated tubes and tips go into a biohazard waste box.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bht3r","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1gig0","text":"Clean all working surfaces and
pipettes with bleach and RNaseZap. Regularly clean gloves with RNaseZap.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":45,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b9ck2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1leae","text":"Keep all the solutions and materials on wet ice. Keep the brain samples on dry ice.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":40,"length":7},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":75,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7pqgq","text":" 
All samples should be handled equally at all steps, as different cell types might have nuclei more or less vulnerable to physical dissociation. All pipetting and resuspension steps should be gentle and consistent to avoid nuclei shearing and clumping.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"601g7","text":"  
Use low-retention tips and tubes for every step where nuclei are involved.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":7,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare solutions on the day of experiment.

Homogenization Buffer maintain at 4 °C Reagent Stock Final Amount-4 Samples (uL) Stock Temp 1 Tris-HCl (pH 7.4) 1M 10mM 120 RT 2 KCl 2M 25mM 150 RT 3 MgCl2 1M 5mM 60 RT 4 DTT 1M 1mM 12 -20ºC 5 RNAse Inhibitor 40U/mL 0.2U/uL 60 -20ºC 6 Protease Inhibitor Dissolve 1 tablet in 600uL nuclease-free water - 2 tablets -20ºC 7 Nuclease-free water - - Fill to 12mL RT RNase Inhibitor Promega Catalog #N2515 Protease Inhibitor Roche Catalog #4693159001

Nuclei Wash Buffer maintain at 4 °C Reagent Stock Final Amount-4 Samples Stock Temp 1 DPBS (Ca2+/Mg2+ free) 1x 1x 28.8mL RT 2 BSA 10% 1% 3.2mL -4ºC 3 RNase Inhibitor 40U/mL 0.2U/uL 160uL -20ºC RNase Inhibitor Promega Catalog #N2515
50% Iodixanol - can be prepped during first spin maintain at 4 °C Reagent Amount-4 Samples (mL) Stock Temp 1 OptiPrep (60% Iodixanol) 6 -4ºC 2 DPBS (Ca2+/Mg2+ Free) 1 RT
25% Iodixanol maintain at 4 °C Reagent Amount-4 Samples (mL) Stock Temp 1 50% Iodixanol 2 -4ºC 2 DPBS (Ca2+/Mg2+ free) 2 RT
Turn on benchtop centrifuge with 2 mL swinging-bucket inserts for spins to 4 °C

Clean 2 mL dounce homogenizer and pestles with bleach, rinse with reagent grade water, wash with RNAseZap, and rinse with Millipore water. Wrap top of dounce homogenizer and bottom of pestles with aluminum foil.
Prepare working solutions go to step #1 Add pre-chilled 500 µL homogenization buffer to the dounce homogenizer and keep on wet ice.
Homogenize brain tissue. Repeat for all tissue samples.
15 strokes On ice with pre-chilled 'A' pestle and 15 with 'B' grinder. Minimize bubbles by not removing the pestle from the liquid while homogenizing.
Transfer brain lysate into 2 mL lo-bind Eppendorf tube.
Add 1 mL homogenization buffer to glass homogenizer and transfer remaining lysate to same Eppendorf tube. Keep tube On ice
Swing bucket centrifuge 500 x g, 4 °C, , 5 minutes
Add 1 mL of 50% Iodixanol to samples. 2 mL 25% Sample/Iodixanol layer. Vortex the iodixanol before pipetting. Weigh tubes to ensure level.
Centrifuge 10000 x g, 4 °C, , 30 minutes with minimal acceleration and deceleration ramp rate.
With a wide-bore P1000 tip, remove myelin debris from top layer of tube. Aspirate supernatant leaving behind nuclei pellet and approximately 500 µL of buffer.
Resuspend with 1.5 mL of Nuclei Wash Buffer. Gently mix by inverting or pipetting
Centrifuge 500 x g, 4 °C, , 5 minutes
Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.
Resuspend nuclei in 1 mL of Nuclei Wash Buffer.
Centrifuge 500 x g, 4 °C, , 5 minutes
Resuspend nuclei in 1 mL 1x dPBS.
Remove 10 µL into PCR strip, stain with 10 µL AO/PI (1:1) and pipette mix. Load 20 µL on K2 slide and count with MGI Nuclei with Debris settings.
Proceed directly to downstream protocol (i.e. nuclei fixation of molecular technology).
Before starting - do the following
Use cell scraper to remove tissue from the 4 100 µm slides. Minimize OCT contamination. Place finger behind slide to warm up tissue and make it easier to scrape.
Use glass Pasteur pipette to remove tissue from cell scraper and place into dounce.
Spin to remove tissue and leave pipette in dounce to soak.
After scraping all 4 slides, rinse inside of glass pipettes with 500 µL homogenization buffer to remove any remaining nuclei.
Carefully aspirate the supernatant. Resuspend each pellet to a final volume of 1 mL in homogenization buffer.
Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.
Pass through 100 µm pluristrainers into new 2 mL lo-bind tube. Wash filter with 200 µL dPBS to remove any additional stuck nuclei. Keep on ice.
creator: Rebecca Chase
